---
title: "Ensuring Data Quality in Large-Scale M&E: Lessons from Urban PSNP Baseline"
author: "Yonatan D. Enaro"
date: "2025-05-08"
categories: [Monitoring & Evaluation, Stata]
---

Best practices for maintaining data integrity across 4,050+ household surveys, 19 health facilities, and 143 communities in urban settings.

## Introduction

Data quality is the cornerstone of credible monitoring and evaluation. In large-scale baseline surveys, ensuring high-quality data requires robust systems, real-time checks, and clear protocols.

## Key Lessons

- Implement high-frequency data quality checks during fieldwork
- Use multi-stage sampling with clear Enumeration Area definitions
- Conduct enumerator training with practical exercises
- Establish feedback loops with supervisors

## Urban PSNP Baseline Experience

The Urban PSNP Baseline survey covered 4,050+ households across 143 communities in Addis Ababa. We employed ODK and Stata for data collection and cleaning, achieving a completion rate of 92%.

```{r}
#| eval: false
#| echo: true
* Data cleaning workflow
use "urban_psnp_baseline.dta", clear
* Check for missing values
misstable summarize
* Outlier detection
summarize consumption, detail
* Harmonize categorical variables
label define yesno 0 "No" 1 "Yes"
```

## Conclusion

Maintaining data quality in large-scale surveys requires proactive measures, not just reactive fixes. Investing in robust systems pays off in reliable, actionable evidence.
